ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: O da yɛli ba ye, “M bɔɔd ye ya keŋi paae m bier Esau san'an pʋ'ʋs o sʋ'ʋŋa tisim, ka yɛl o ye, ‘Fʋ tʋmtʋm Jakob da bɛnɛ Laban san'an yuug hali nɛ zina.
translated: "He told them, saying, "You shall say this to my brother Esau: 'Your servant Jacob has been staying with Laban until now."

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Amaa fʋ yɛl ye fʋ na tʋm tʋʋm sʋma tisim, ka kɛ ka m yaas an wʋʋ atɛuk titanbin'isʋg nɛ, linɛ ka sɔ' kʋ nyaŋi kaalɛ.”
translated: But if you refuse to let my people go, then tomorrow I will bring locusts into your territory, and they will cover the face of the earth so that no one can see the ground.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jakob da gbɛɛn anina yʋ'ʋŋ kan la nie bɛog, ka yis on mɔr si'el la sieba ye o tʋm tis o bier Esau.
translated: Jacob had now become an old man with children, and he wanted his son who was younger than his brother Esau to receive his blessing.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: ka o mɛŋ kpɛlim o kɔn'ɔkɔn'. Ka dau sɔ' kena ka ba mɔ nɛ taaba ka bɛog ti bɔɔd nier.
translated: and you should love your neighbor. And a person should not be proud and think that people are not equal.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Ka dau la ye, “Fʋ yʋ'ʋr kʋ lɛm buon ye Jakob ya'asɛ, amaa Israel, bɔzugɔ fʋ mɔ nɛ fʋ Wina'am nɛ dap ka nyaŋ.”
translated: He said to him, "Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with your God and with men and have prevailed."

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ka Jakob yɛl ye, “M sɔsidif nɛ, yɛlimi fʋ yʋ'ʋr!” Ka o lɛbis ye, “Ka bɔ ka fʋ bu'osidi m yʋ'ʋrɛ?” Ka niŋ o zug bareka anina.
translated: And Jacob said, "My lord, tell me your name!" And he answered, "Why do you ask my name?" And there his blessing came upon him.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Ka Jakob yii ani ken ka Wina'am maliaknam sia'al o.
translated: And Jacob did thus and Laban's daughters became his.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Jakob da pʋn dɛŋi tʋm nidib Esau san'an Seir teŋin dinɛ bɛ Edom sʋ'ʋlim la.
translated: Jacob sent messengers ahead to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.

translated: The days and years passed and Jacob's children all grew up and had their own children, and there were goats and sheep and cattle in abundance.

translated: "Then you will say to him, 'They belong to your servant Jacob; it is a gift sent to my lord Esau; and he himself is also behind us.'"

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.

translated: When the day broke and he did not prevail over him, he touched Jacob's hip joint so that it became dislocated.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Ka winnigi da dʋt na la ka o gaad Peniel ka kpɛ'ɛsid; o kpiilʋg la zug.
translated: And the sun rose upon him as he passed Peniel and he was limping; on his thigh.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.

translated: And it happened that when Israel was dwelling in that land, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah his father's concubine, and Israel heard about it.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”

translated: And Esau said, "Let us go! I will accompany you."

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Amaa Jakob lɛbis ye, “Ayei, m zugdaana! Fʋ mi' ye biis la nam pɔɔd, ka li mɛ sʋm ye m gɔsid pɛ'ɛs nɛ na'abanɛ nan mu'asidi ba biis la. Ti ya'a ye ti mugusi ba keŋ baa daar yinne, bʋnkɔnbid la na kpi wʋsa.
translated: But Jacob answered, "No, my lord! If you are pleased with me, accept this gift from me. For to see your face is like seeing the face of God, now that you have received me favorably. Please accept the present that was brought to you, for God has been gracious to me and I have all I need." And because Jacob insisted, Esau accepted it.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: M bierɛ, fʋn gaadim tuon ka mam mɛ kpɛlim nya'aŋi dɔl bʋnkɔnbid la bi'el-bi'el, nɛ biis la, hali nɛ tinamɛ na paae fʋ san'an Seir teŋin na.”
translated: My son, you know how I have served you with all my strength and diligently, and the children also, even though the land will pass to your brother Seir.

ref: So Esau left for Edom.
text: Ka Esau gban'e o suori lɛb Seir teŋin.
translated: And Esau took his wives to the land of Seir.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Jakob n yi Padanaram la nya'aŋ la o da paae Sekɛm tempʋʋgin dinɛ bɛ Keenan sʋ'ʋlimin la nɛ laafi. Ka yi kpikpɛŋ bi'ela n zin'in anina.
translated: Jacob came from Padanaram and arrived at the city of Shechem which is in the land of Canaan safely. And he camped outside the city.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.

translated: If you had stayed in the land with them, you would not have seen any good thing that would be like this."

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.

translated: When they reached the place where they usually met the old woman, his friend met Daina there too. But he was the one who his heart told him to greet his mother-in-law.

translated: And all the men who went out of the city gate listened to Hamor and Shechem his son and all the males were circumcised, all who went out of the city gate.

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.

translated: And they gave to him all the foreign gods that were in their hands and the rings that were in their ears, and Jacob hid them under the oak tree that was near Shechem.

ref: After Jacob came back to the land of Canaan, God appeared to him again. This time he gave Jacob a new name and blessed him by saying: I am God All-Powerful, and from now on your name will be Israel instead of Jacob. You will have many children. Your descendants will become nations, and some of the men in your family will even be kings.
text: Ka Wina'am lɛn yɛl o ye, “Manɛ an Wina'am Onɛ Galis la. Du'ami biis ka nɔbigi bɛdegʋ. Fʋ na wiak buudi bɛdegʋ hali ka ba lieb tembɛda. Na'anam na yii fʋ tuŋʋn na.
translated: And God said to him, "I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply. A nation and a company of nations shall come from you. Kings shall come from your body."

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.

translated: And Jacob took a stone and put it under his head, and it was very hard for Rachel's sake.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.

translated: Esau took his wives and his sons and his daughters and all the people of his household and his livestock and all his animals and all his possessions which he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went to another country away from his brother Jacob.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

translated: Esau's wife Oholibama bore him three sons: Jeush and Jalam and Korah. These were the children of Esau's wife Oholibama who was Ana's daughter in the land of Canaan.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Baal-Hanan onɛ an Akibo biig la da kpi la, Hada da di'e o zin'igi di na'am la, ka o tempʋʋg la yʋ'ʋr buon Pau, ka o pu'a yʋ'ʋr buon Mehetabɛl ka o ma yʋ'ʋr buon Matired onɛ mɛ an Mezahab pu'ayua.
translated: When Baal-Hanan son of Akibor died, Hadar succeeded him as king, and his city's name was Pau, and his wife's name was Mehetabel and her mother's name was Matired who was the daughter of Mezahab.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Israel da nɔŋ Josef n gaadi o biis la wʋsa, bɔzugɔ o da du'a o nɛ saŋkanɛ ka o da kʋdig. Ka o da sɛn fukanɛ bɛɛ li kɔn' ka vɛnl hali tis o.
translated: Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he had borne him in his old age. And he made him a long robe with sleeves.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.

translated: And if you tell your husband this and you tell your family members, and your husband divorces you and tells you, "Why did you reveal the secret and you are disgraced? I and you and your family members will not be able to lift our faces again?"

translated: They had prepared the funeral and buried their father, and one of them said, "Our father is dead now, what if our brother still hates us because of what we did to him?"

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.

translated: And you will ask those people there, "Where is the shrine prostitute who was at Enaim by the roadside?" And they will tell you, "There has been no shrine prostitute here."

translated: And he left Judah and told him, "I did not find her, and also the men of that place said, 'There has never been a shrine prostitute here.'"

translated: When they brought Tamar, she sent a message to her father-in-law saying, "I am pregnant by the man who owns these things that are here." And she said, "Examine carefully whose seal and cord these are. They belong to him, and the staff."
